scenario_name: Scenario 19 — Circular Dependency in Agent Outputs
justification: Hits multiple fail criteria. The circular dependency chain (Bird -> MJ -> Pippen -> Bird) was never identified — the output synthesizes all three agent outputs as if they are largely self-contained and coherent. The escalations array is empty; no escalation to Coach K was made. The agent invented missing behavior by recommending 'fail-closed default (reject the request with 401/403)' rather than flagging that no agent defined this. The brief is addressed to Shaq and explicitly directs Shaq to handle the undefined cases ('Shaq should implement a fail-closed default', 'Shaq should evaluate whether AsyncLocal is needed'). The foundational gap around missing/invalid tenant context handling is mentioned only as an open question, not as a blocking circular assumption across all three agents.

observations.text: Circular dependency completely undetected — no mention of the Bird->MJ->Pippen->Bird assumption loop
Escalations array is empty despite a clear blocking circular dependency requiring Coach K intervention
Invented error handling behavior: 'Shaq should implement a fail-closed default (reject the request with 401/403)' — exactly the failure mode the rubric warns about
Brief produced and addressed to Shaq, directing him to resolve the undefined behaviors rather than escalating
The three specific gaps (valid context definition, error response, async failure handling) are not all named as undefined foundational behaviors
Did note in open_questions that Bird did not define missing/invalid context behavior — partial awareness of one gap
Correctly summarized the happy-path architecture that IS defined (JWT extraction, DI injection, job payload propagation)
Terminology alignment and domain rules are well-structured for the parts that were actually defined

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1

trials.justification: Fails on multiple pass criteria. (1) Circular dependency chain (Bird->MJ->Pippen->Bird) is never identified — the output notes individual gaps but does not trace the assumption loop. (2) The escalations array is empty — no escalation to Coach K was made despite foundational unknowns. (3) Error handling behavior was invented: acceptance criteria includes 'rejected with an appropriate error (e.g., 401/403)' which is exactly the behavior no agent defined. (4) A full handoff brief was produced and routed to Shaq, with open_questions asking Shaq to 'clarify' the undefined cases — a listed failure mode. The output identifies some gaps in open_questions and low_confidence_areas, but treats them as footnotes on an otherwise complete brief rather than as blockers requiring escalation.

observations.text: Circular dependency chain completely undetected — output never traces the Bird->MJ->Pippen->Bird assumption loop, only notes that Bird didn't define failure behavior
Escalations array is empty despite foundational gaps — no escalation to Coach K was made
Invented missing behavior: acceptance criterion 'Reject requests with missing tenant context... rejected with an appropriate error (e.g., 401/403)' fabricates exactly what was undefined
Full handoff brief produced and routed to Shaq, treating undefined behaviors as open questions rather than blockers
Open questions direct Shaq to 'clarify' missing pieces — a listed failure mode (routing to Shaq to define missing behavior)
Correctly identifies that Bird did not define missing/invalid context behavior and that Pippen assumed Bird would cover it
Happy-path synthesis is accurate — JWT source, DI injection, job-payload propagation, logging enrichment are all correctly captured
Confidence level of 72 and low_confidence_areas show awareness that something is incomplete, but awareness without escalation is insufficient
Only two of three specific gaps named — async context inheritance failure handling is not called out as a distinct undefined behavior

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1

trials.justification: Circular dependency chain (Bird -> MJ -> Pippen -> Bird) was not detected. The agent explicitly dismissed the inter-agent assumptions as 'complementary layers of the same design... No actual conflict' in contradictions_resolved. No escalation was raised (escalations array is empty). A full handoff brief was produced and routed to Shaq, treating the three outputs as self-contained and complete. The agent invented missing behavior: acceptance criteria include 'rejected with 401/403' for missing context, and open_questions direct Shaq to 'implement a fail-closed default.' The gap around missing/invalid context handling is mentioned in open_questions and low_confidence_areas but attributed only to Bird ('Bird did not define...'), missing the circular nature where all three agents assumed someone else would define it.

observations.text: Circular dependency completely missed — contradictions_resolved explicitly says 'No actual conflict' and frames the three agent outputs as complementary, which is the primary failure mode
Empty escalations array despite a scenario that fundamentally requires escalation to resolve circular assumptions
Full handoff brief produced and sent to Shaq, treating incomplete inputs as synthesizable
Invented error handling behavior: acceptance criterion specifies '401/403' rejection, and open_questions tell Shaq to 'implement a fail-closed default'

Shaq directed to handle undefined cases: 'Shaq should implement a fail-closed default but Bird should confirm' — this is exactly the anti-pattern of routing implementation of undefined behavior to the coder
The gap around missing/invalid tenant context handling IS noted in open_questions and low_confidence_areas, showing partial awareness of the problem
What IS defined (JWT source, log requirement, job payload inheritance) is accurately summarized
Only one of three circular links identified — notes Bird didn't define error handling, but does not trace MJ's assumption about Pippen or Pippen's assumption about Bird

grader_results.detail: field escalations has 0 items, below min_items 1
